license: Public Domain (Sobotta Atlas of Human Anatomy, 1911)
title: Nerves and Vessels of the Ulnar Side of the Elbow
topic: ulnar nerve pathway and medial elbow anatomy
description: A classic anatomical drawing detailing the medial aspect of the elbow, specifically highlighting the pathway of the ulnar nerve as it passes posterior to the medial epicondyle through the cubital tunnel. The diagram also illustrates the median nerve and brachial artery descending anteriorly into the cubital fossa.
clinical_notes: The ulnar nerve is highly vulnerable to compression or traction at the cubital tunnel (Cubital Tunnel Syndrome). This results in paresthesia of the ring and small fingers, and late-stage intrinsic hand muscle atrophy (clawing). OT interventions include fabricating an anterior elbow extension orthosis (worn nocturnally at 30-45 degrees of flexion) to prevent nerve stretching, teaching ulnar nerve gliding exercises, and providing ergonomic education to avoid prolonged elbow flexion or resting on hard surfaces.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 11 – The Muscular System & Chapter 13 – The Peripheral Nervous System
